etymology.description: Originally the same character as 需(需) (xū, "to need") -> characters diverged during Han dynasty
etymology.interpretation: 耎(耎) (ruǎn, "soft; weak") shares origins with 需(需) (xū, "to need") where the divergence led to its distinct meaning of softness or weakness, emphasizing a more flexible or yielding nature, compared to 需(需) which focuses on requisites or needs.
